author: han0110
jsign
content:
I think prob being human readable isn't strictly necessary, but might be  convenient. e.g. the `hash(... | | ... | ...)` sounds enough for identifying, but only looking at that hash might be quite opaque for humans to identify and track compared to at least the `ethrex-<commit>/zisk-vX.X.X` style. The semver independent versioning for guest and zkvm might be a futher stretch to show compatibility combinations.

So I think the minimum is being sure each combination is uniquely identifiable; then each further option seems like an extra convenience thing to make it nicier for humans only but not strictly necessary. 

I think for a while we could rely on something like  `ethrex-<commit>/zisk-vX.X.X` since requires no coordination and no other new standard that ELs and zkVMs must follow like in semver (since they must follow the semver standard, not us).  But we can still keep the semver proposal around and wait maybe having more their feedback, and also potentially other actors e.g. pandaops if they feel this can facilitate operations reg which els and zkvms things can be combined (so more like the prover operator side).

no strong opinion here
